explanation: This LTL formula states that, whenever an alarm is generated by DM1, it must be the case that within 1 time unit all the other DMs in the network (DM2, DM3, DM4, and DM5) receive the alarm. Here, bool_exp3 is not needed, and is set to TRUE to indicate no restriction from that expression. This fully captures the requirement in the provided natural language sentence, which stipulates that once a DM generates an alarm, all other DMs receive it within 1 time unit.

This LTL property expresses that whenever an alarm is generated by DM1, it must be the case that within 1 time unit, DM2, DM3, DM4, and DM5 will each receive the alarm. This aligns with the specification that when a DM generates an alarm, all other DMs in the network receive it within 1 time unit.

The property requires that whenever an alarm is generated by DM1 (representing a DM generating an alarm), it is followed within 1 time unit by the alarm being received by each of the other DMs (DM2, DM3, DM4, DM5). The chosen template expresses that after the triggering condition (alarm generation by DM1), the required events (alarm receipt by other DMs) must all occur within the specified time interval. This matches the meaning of the natural-language requirement: 'The alarm signal generated by a DM is received by the other DMs belonging to the network within 1 time unit.'

This LTL property expresses that whenever an alarm is generated by DM1, then within 1 time unit, all other DMs in the network (DM2, DM3, DM4, DM5) must receive the alarm. This captures the requirement in the input natural language that the alarm signal generated by one DM must be received by all other DMs within 1 time unit. The implication assures the causality from generation to reception, the time-bounded 'finally' operator guarantees the time constraint, and the conjunction ensures all recipients are included.

The property specifies that if an alarm is generated by one DM, then within 1 time unit, each of the other DMs (DM2, DM3, DM4, and DM5) must have received the alarm. This matches the requirement from the natural language: 'the alarm signal generated by a DM is received by the other DMs belonging to the network within 1 time unit.' Here, the property requires that whenever 'alarm_generated_dm1' becomes true (i.e., DM1 generates the alarm), within 1 time unit, all other DMs ('alarm_received_dm2', 'alarm_received_dm3', 'alarm_received_dm4', and 'alarm_received_dm5') must have received the alarm. The LTL template chosen expresses that globally, whenever the alarm is generated, we must guarantee (within the specified time bound) that the other DMs receive the alarm.

The natural language specifies that whenever a DM generates an alarm, all other DMs in the network must receive it within 1 time unit. Using the atomic proposition 'alarm_generated_dm1' for alarm generation and 'alarm_received_dm2', 'alarm_received_dm3', 'alarm_received_dm4', 'alarm_received_dm5' for receipt at the other DMs, we can interpret this as: whenever 'alarm_generated_dm1' becomes true, then within the next 1 time unit, all receipt signals ('alarm_received_dm2', ..., 'alarm_received_dm5') must hold continuously. The most fitting template for this strict time-bounded guarantee for each occurrence is template 146: G (bool_exp3 -> G[0,N_DURATION1] bool_exp4), where bool_exp3 is the generation and bool_exp4 is the conjunction of all receipt APs. N_DURATION1 is set to 1.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (globally) that if an alarm is generated by DM1, then within 1 time unit, the alarm is received by all other DMs (DM2, DM3, DM4, DM5). This captures the requirement that whenever an alarm signal is generated by a DM, all other DMs in the network must receive it within one time unit. The expression is in the standard boolean form for the LTL pattern: bool_exp1 -> G[0,1] bool_exp3, with bool_exp1 = alarm_generated_dm1 and bool_exp3 = (alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5).

The property formalizes that whenever an alarm is generated by DM1, then within 1 time unit, the alarm should be received by all other DMs in the network (DM2, DM3, DM4, DM5). This is formulated as a response property: whenever 'alarm_generated_dm1' is true (the triggering event), then within 1 time unit the boolean expression (alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5) must hold, meaning all those DMs have received the alarm. No placeholders are left empty; all boolean expressions are valid.

The input requires that when an alarm is generated by a DM (let's take DM1), the other DMs in the network (here: DM2, DM3, DM4, DM5) receive that alarm signal within 1 time unit. We model this by requiring that "whenever an alarm is generated by DM1, then within 1 time unit alarms must be received by DM2, DM3, DM4, and DM5." The LTL template that directly enforces this is the one that uses the globally operator with the implication: if the alarm is generated by DM1, in the next 1 time unit each of 'alarm_received_dm2', 'alarm_received_dm3', 'alarm_received_dm4', and 'alarm_received_dm5' must happen, i.e. globally (alarm_generated_dm1 implies that, in the next 1 time unit, all other DMs receive it). Thus we use template 70. The boolean expression for 'bool_exp3' is 'alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5' to require all DMs receive the alarm.

This LTL property states that whenever an alarm is generated by DM1 (alarm_generated_dm1), it must be the case that within 1 time unit all other DMs (DM2, DM3, DM4, and DM5) have received the alarm signal. This is modeled as a strict bounded response: 'G (alarm_generated_dm1 -> ((!(!(alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5))) U[0,1] (alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5)))'.

Here, bool_exp1 is 'alarm_generated_dm1', representing the triggering condition. bool_exp3 is '!(alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5)', which is true until all DMs have received the alarm. bool_exp4 is 'alarm_received_dm2 & alarm_received_dm3 & alarm_received_dm4 & alarm_received_dm5', representing the reception of the alarm by all DMs. N_DURATION1 is set to 1 to enforce the 1 time unit bound. This property directly fulfills the requirement stated in the natural language input.
